payload.description: A bus-mounted camera system that issues violations to vehicles occupying bus lanes, to double parked vehicles along bus routes, and to vehicles blocking bus stops. Administered in partnership between the MTA, NYC Department of Finance (DOF), and NYC Department of Transportation (DOT). The 2023 NYS Legislature passed a provision in the budget to expand ACE. Currently active on 60 bus routes.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The 2023 NYS Legislature passed a provision in the budget to expand Automated Camera Enforcement, or ACE. The ACE program is a bus-mounted camera system that issues violations to vehicles occupying bus lanes, to double parked vehicles along bus routes, and to vehicles blocking bus stops.
The program is administered in partnership between the MTA, the New York City Department of Finance (DOF) and the New York City Department of Transportation (DOT).
It is currently active on 60 bus routes

payload.raw_value_text: over one million average weekday riders

evidence_refs.source_quote: benefiting over one million average weekday riders

payload.metric_name: bus speed improvement on ACE segments
payload.raw_value_text: some segments on these routes achieving gains of nearly 30%

evidence_refs.source_quote: camera enforcement has helped improve bus speeds across the full length of ACE-enabled routes, with some segments on these routes achieving gains of nearly 30%

payload.raw_value_text: an average of 10% of violators receiving multiple tickets

evidence_refs.source_quote: With an average of 10% of violators receiving multiple tickets
Fines start at $50 and increase by $50 for each additional violation received, up to a maximum of $250 per violation
Fines start at $50 and increase by $50 for each additional violation received, up to a maximum of $250 per violation

tool_args.label: 98% of New Yorkers live within 1/4 mile of bus stop

payload.raw_value_text: 98% of New Yorkers live within 1/4 mile of a bus stop

evidence_refs.source_quote: 98% of New Yorkers live within 1/4 mile of a bus stop

payload.description: The 2023 NYS Legislature passed a provision in the budget to expand Automated Camera Enforcement (ACE)

evidence_refs.source_quote: The 2023 NYS Legislature passed a provision in the budget to expand Automated Camera Enforcement, or ACE.

payload.claim_text: On each ACE bus route, the MTA and City issue warning notices for 60 days before fine-bearing violations begin.
payload.description: Warning period policy for ACE enforcement

evidence_refs.source_quote: On each ACE bus route, the MTA and City issue warning notices for 60 days before fine-bearing violations begin.

payload.claim_text: Obstructing active bus lanes and bus stops and double parking is illegal 24/7.
payload.description: ACE violation applies around the clock

evidence_refs.source_quote: Obstructing active bus lanes and bus stops and double parking is illegal 24/7.

payload.claim_text: Vehicles committing the above illegal acts for the above specified respective times are issued violations only after being observed by two buses.
payload.description: Violations require observation by two buses

evidence_refs.source_quote: Vehicles committing the above illegal acts for the above specified respective times are issued violations only after being observed by two buses.

payload.claim_text: Fines start at $50 and increase by $50 for each additional violation received, up to a maximum of $250 per violation.
payload.description: Escalating fine structure for ACE violations

evidence_refs.source_quote: Fines start at $50 and increase by $50 for each additional violation received, up to a maximum of $250 per violation.

tool_args.label: Violations issued once every two hours

payload.claim_text: Violations will be issued once every two hours for illegal double parking and for illegally obstructing bus lanes and bus stops.
payload.description: ACE violations are issued once every two hours per vehicle

evidence_refs.source_quote: Violations will be issued once every two hours for illegal double parking and for illegally obstructing bus lanes and bus stops.

payload.claim_text: ACE violations are civil penalties and do not affect your driving record or insurance rates.

evidence_refs.source_quote: ACE violations are civil penalties and do not affect your driving record or insurance rates.

payload.claim_text: Each violation is reviewed for accuracy by a City employee before being issued.
payload.description: Accuracy review process for ACE violations

evidence_refs.source_quote: Each violation is reviewed for accuracy by a City employee before being issued.

payload.claim_text: All revenue from ACE violations supports MTA operations, including improved bus service.

evidence_refs.source_quote: All revenue from ACE violations supports MTA operations, including improved bus service.

payload.claim_text: Vehicles standing in a bus lane for longer than 5 minutes and are observed by two buses are issued an violation.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Vehicles standing in a bus lane for longer than 5 minutes and are observed by two buses are issued an violation.

payload.claim_text: Vehicles standing in or obstructing access to bus stops for longer than 2.5 minutes and are observed by two buses are issued a violation.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Vehicles standing in or obstructing access to bus stops for longer than 2.5 minutes and are observed by two buses are issued a violation.

payload.claim_text: Vehicles that are found to be double parked for longer than 2.5 minutes and are observed by two buses are issued a violation.
payload.description: Double parking violation threshold on ACE-enforced roadways

evidence_refs.source_quote: Vehicles that are found to be double parked for longer than 2.5 minutes and are observed by two buses are issued a violation.
Updated May 15, 2026
